reasoning: The value on 2026-01-31 (the likely baseline for 2026-02-01) is 6.66, which is a local maximum due to a mechanical month-end spike (likely driven by the removal of 0-yielding cash that accumulated during the month). During the month, accumulating cash mechanically pulls the effective yield down by a few basis points, and 6.66 is already elevated compared to the mid-6.40s seen earlier in January. This makes it less likely (around 30%) that the value will be strictly higher on February 8, especially given the minor mechanical headwind of intra-month cash accumulation and natural mean reversion from the localized high. For March 3, which is 1 month away, market volatility plays a much larger role and effectively washes out most of the short-term mechanical effects. Since uncertainty should scale with time and the underlying economic trend is relatively flat with low spreads, the probability naturally reverts toward 50%. The linear regression tool provided an objective P(>6.66) = 0.25, which provides a strong anchor for the short-term forecast. Thus, I estimate a 30% chance of an increase by Feb 8 and a 45% chance of an increase by March 3.
